### TreeLearn vs. Treeiso

::: {#fig-tree_instance_seg fig-scap='TreeLearn vs. TreeIso'}

::: {layout-ncol=3 layout-nrow=2}

![TreeLearn [@henrichTreeLearndeep2024]](../2_1_figures/tree_instance_seg/TreeLearn.png)

![TreeLearn [@henrichTreeLearndeep2024]](../2_1_figures/tree_instance_seg/TreeLearn2.png)

![TreeLearn [@henrichTreeLearndeep2024]](../2_1_figures/tree_instance_seg/TreeLearnTop.png)

![TreeIso [@xi3DGraphBased2022]](../2_1_figures/tree_instance_seg/TreeIso.png)

![TreeIso [@xi3DGraphBased2022]](../2_1_figures/tree_instance_seg/TreeIso2.png)

![TreeIso [@xi3DGraphBased2022]](../2_1_figures/tree_instance_seg/TreeIsoTop.png)

:::

Comparison between extracted trees of TreeLearn and TreeIso. For both, ground normalized pointclouds used and noise was removed using Cloudcompare. TreeIso additionally removed bottom 1.5m of vegetation as preprocessing step.
:::

See @tbl-panel for details, especially @tbl-a.


Drawbacks:

- models are developed for managed/structured forests (mainly coniferous trees)
- models are develope based on TLS not UAV platforms (e.g. much more below canopy level information) --> there is a highly advanced one by @xiangAutomatedforest2024 which is UAV based, but also in coniferous managed forest (I doubt its worth the effort)



Next step --> compare to @zornerPyCrownFast2018 (CHM/watershed approach) and/or @zhongIndividualTree2024 (DL on RGB/CHM) for raster based tree instance segmentation.

## Notes

### Important traits of MR
 - pustules are on leaf underside
 - yearly maximum in March & April [@beresfordImpactsmyrtle2019]

 - Summer 24/25 had low symptoms due to weather conditions (R. Beresford, Plant & Food Research, pers. comm.)


### Ideas

- wrap multispectral information onto lidar data
